title: cli-launch.always-buffer-openfile
description: handleOpenFile must always write pendingURL first, then also post the notification. Whichever path wins (drain on appear vs receive notification), the URL lands. Fixes 'peek <folder>' opening blank on cold start.
close_reason: handleOpenFile now always writes to LaunchURLBuffer.shared (observable @Published). MainWindow observes via @ObservedObject + .onChange so a late-arriving folder URL still opens, regardless of SwiftUI subscriber attach order. AppDelegate tests updated, regression test added (testColdStartWithDidFinishLaunchingBeforeOpenFile).

title: tree-perf.ignore-noise
description: Default-skip .git, .build, node_modules, .venv, dist, target, __pycache__, .next, .turbo. Massive practical win in real repos. 'Show all files' mode opts back in.
close_reason: FolderBrowser is now lazy: only walks one level on open(); children of subdirectories load on expand via loadChildren(at:). ignoredDirNames skips node_modules, __pycache__, target, dist, build, venv, vendor, out in md-only mode. Tests updated.

title: tree-perf.lazy-children
description: Only walk one level on FolderBrowser.open(); populate children lazily as the user expands directories. Eliminates beach-ball on big repos (node_modules etc.).
close_reason: FolderBrowser is now lazy: only walks one level on open(); children of subdirectories load on expand via loadChildren(at:). ignoredDirNames skips node_modules, __pycache__, target, dist, build, venv, vendor, out in md-only mode. Tests updated.

title: peek <folder>/peek . silently drops folder arg on macOS 26
description:
On at least one user's machine, peek 0.4.2 receives a folder arg via the shim (open -a peek .) but the window opens with no folder loaded. Reproduced on Darwin 25.x dev box: launching .build/peek.app with /tmp shows no fds opened into /tmp.

Root cause: AppDelegate only implements legacy application(_:openFile:). On macOS 13+ Apple recommends application(_:open: [URL]) and on macOS 26 (Tahoe / Darwin 25) the legacy callback may not fire when the modern one is available. Result: folder argument is silently dropped.

Fix: implement application(_:open:) to drain the URL array into LaunchURLBuffer; keep openFile as a fallback for older callers. Verify with both 'peek .' and 'peek /some/folder' from a fresh shell.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.4.3: added application(_:open:) callback. macOS 26 (Darwin 25) silently dropped folder args because Apple's modern openURLs callback takes precedence over legacy openFile when both are advertised by NSApplicationDelegate — we only implemented the legacy one. New handleOpenURL drains URL into LaunchURLBuffer, sharing the same code path as openFile. Verified end-to-end: 'open -a peek /tmp' now produces an open fd on /private/tmp (FolderBrowser successfully walked it). 3 new tests in AppDelegateTests cover modern callback paths; 125 total passing.

title: v0.5 epic: data viewers — JSON, JSONL, search
description:
Roadmap epic: peek extends its motto ("agents don't read .docx") from Markdown to the whole structured-text surface agents emit. v0.5.0 ships JSON + JSONL together with: collapsible tree, syntax-highlighted leaves, theme-aware, key-path copy on click, in-tree substring search across keys+values, and virtualized rows so 100MB JSONL is still lightning-fast. Same peek shell — sidebar, breadcrumb, theme toggle, zoom, find-bar reused or extended. v0.5.0 must keep the 'light, native, beautiful' promise: no spinner on open of large files, no memory bloat regression from v0.4.2.

Child beads track the slices.

title: Investigate and reduce memory footprint (target <150MB idle)
description:
Reported live: peek is consuming 1GB+ of memory during normal use. This directly violates the 'light' promise (one of the three product pillars: light, native, beautiful). For a Markdown viewer at idle / single-document use, a reasonable ceiling is ~150MB RSS; folder-open with FSEvents + FileIndex might justify 250-400MB on huge trees, but never 1GB+.

Likely suspects to investigate, in order:

1. WKWebView retention — MarkdownWebView may not be releasing the underlying WKWebView / WKProcessPool when documents close. WKWebView has notoriously heavy per-instance cost; if we leak even a couple across reloads we burn hundreds of MB. Check whether shell() creates a fresh view per render vs reusing one, and whether reload paths re-instantiate.

2. Renderer caching — if we cache rendered HTML strings or image data per document and never evict, large docs (or many reloaded docs) will accumulate. Check Renderer for any Dictionary/Cache that grows unbounded.

3. FileIndex — for huge folders the in-memory index of URLs + basenames could be sizable. Check whether FileIndex retains String copies of every path or just URLs, and whether it dedupes when re-walked. Cross-check against ContentSearcher results retention.

4. FolderBrowser loadedChildren cache — lazy load is good but we never evict. For a vibecoding session that walks many subtrees, this cache grows monotonically. Consider an LRU bound or eviction on collapse.

5. Image / asset retention in rendered Markdown — if the WebView caches images aggressively and we render a doc with many large images, RSS spikes.

6. Reload churn — FSEvents-driven rebuild may be allocating without releasing. Check generation-counter path for retained closures.

Acceptance:
- Reproduce: open the same folder/file the user saw 1GB on, capture Instruments Allocations trace, identify top allocators.
- Fix: drop idle RSS below 150MB for a single-doc view, below 400MB for a 10k-file folder open.
- Add a CI-friendly smoke (or at least a manual checklist) so we catch regressions before shipping a release.
close_reason: Fixed in v0.4.2: WKWebView hot-reload path now (1) caches base.css/hljs-light.css/hljs-dark.css/highlight.min.js statically (~1MB no longer re-read+re-allocated per save), (2) swaps <main> innerHTML in place via JS when only the body changes, instead of nuking the page with loadHTMLString, (3) uses .nonPersistent() WKWebsiteDataStore so cookies/cache/localStorage don't accrete across launches. Verified: phys_footprint after 30 hot-reload cycles = 39MB current / 98MB peak (target was <150MB idle). Tests: 122 passing including 6 new in MarkdownWebViewHotReloadTests.

title: default-handler.first-launch-prompt
description: On first launch (or on first .md open), if peek is not the system default handler for net.daringfireball.markdown, prompt the user once: 'Set peek as default for Markdown?' / 'Not now'. Persist the asked flag so we don't nag. Add a File menu item to set explicitly later.
close_reason: DefaultHandler.isPeekDefault / setPeekAsDefault wrap LS APIs. MainWindow shows a one-time SwiftUI alert on first launch when peek is not the default for net.daringfireball.markdown; choice persists via Pref.askedDefaultHandler. File menu has 'Set peek as Default for Markdown' for explicit invocation. 4 new tests on the constants + pref state machine; LS itself isn't testable without mutating the user's defaults.

title: cli-launch.repro-test
description: Unit-test handleDidFinishLaunching + handleOpenFile ordering with a folder URL, both orderings, to lock the regression.
close_reason: handleOpenFile now always writes to LaunchURLBuffer.shared (observable @Published). MainWindow observes via @ObservedObject + .onChange so a late-arriving folder URL still opens, regardless of SwiftUI subscriber attach order. AppDelegate tests updated, regression test added (testColdStartWithDidFinishLaunchingBeforeOpenFile).

title: cli-launch.observe-pending
description: MainWindow should observe pendingURL changes (not just consume on .onAppear), so a late-arriving folder URL still opens.
close_reason: handleOpenFile now always writes to LaunchURLBuffer.shared (observable @Published). MainWindow observes via @ObservedObject + .onChange so a late-arriving folder URL still opens, regardless of SwiftUI subscriber attach order. AppDelegate tests updated, regression test added (testColdStartWithDidFinishLaunchingBeforeOpenFile).

title: tree-perf.off-main-rebuild
description: Move FSEvents-driven rebuild off the main queue; hop back with the result. Cheap with lazy children but the right discipline.
close_reason: rebuildAsync() runs FSEvents-driven refresh on a serial bg queue (peek.folderbrowser.rebuild). Hops back to main with results via Task @MainActor; discards if rootURL/showAllFiles changed mid-flight. Initial open and showAllFiles toggle stay sync (one level, sub-ms). New tests: testRefreshAppliesNewChildrenOffMain, testRefreshAfterCloseIsNoop.

title: fs-watch.fsevents-watcher
description: Add FSEvents-based recursive folder watcher so the sidebar tree updates automatically when files change on disk (high-speed vibecoding flows). Native, low-overhead, replaces the need for polling.
close_reason: Implemented FSEvents-based FolderWatcher; FolderBrowser now auto-refreshes on disk changes. Cmd+Shift+R wired as manual fallback.

title: data-viewer.tests
description: Test surface: (a) JSON parser handles spec edge cases (deeply nested, unicode, large numbers, escaped strings); (b) parse errors carry line/col; (c) tree flattening matches expected indices through expand/collapse cycles; (d) key-path generation matches both dotted + pointer formats; (e) search returns matches in document order; (f) JSONL line-offset index round-trips against linewise reads. Perf-budget tests like v0.4 SearchPerfTests: 50MB JSON parse <1s, 100k-node flatten <50ms, 1GB JSONL line-index <500ms.

title: data-viewer.keypath-copy
description: Right-click or click a JSON node → copy its key path to clipboard. Two formats: dotted ('foo.bar[0].baz') and JSON-pointer ('/foo/bar/0/baz'). Settings later; in v0.5 default to dotted with an option-modifier for JSON-pointer. Show a brief toast on copy. Critical for the 'agents emit, you debug' workflow: paste the path back into a tool / agent prompt.

title: data-viewer.search
description: In-tree substring search: type \"foo\" and we highlight matches in keys, string values, and number/bool literals. Cmd+G / Cmd+Shift+G to step through. For JSON: walk the tree; for JSONL: walk visible lines first, then expand search to off-screen lines progressively (don't block UI for 1GB). Reuses the existing FindBar shell so the keystroke is the familiar Cmd+F.

title: data-viewer.jsonl-line-virtualization
description: JSONL = one JSON value per line. Treat each line as an independent collapsible tree entry, but virtualize at the *line* level too: only parse + render visible lines, lazy-parse below. Target: 1GB JSONL opens in <500ms (first paint), scrolls smoothly without parsing everything. Use line-byte-offset index built once on open (mmap or chunked read); per-line parse on demand.

title: data-viewer.tree-virtualization
description: Render only visible rows. Compute a flat list of visible nodes (collapsed parents hide their descendants); recompute on expand/collapse. Re-render incrementally on scroll. Target: 100k-node JSON tree scrolls at 60fps and uses <100MB extra RSS. Decision point: WebView-rendered (reuses existing pipeline, easy theming) vs native NSTableView/SwiftUI List (probably faster but new code path). Lead with WebView + JS virtualization to share the v0.4.2 hot-reload infra; benchmark before committing.

title: data-viewer.tree-render
description: Collapsible tree UI for a parsed JSONValue. Each node: indented row with toggle chevron for containers, key on the left, value (or summary like '{42}' / '[12]') on the right. Theme-aware via existing peek CSS variables. Single-click chevron to expand/collapse; option-click to expand all under cursor. Keep typography matching the Markdown view (same monospace for code-like content).

title: data-viewer.json-parser
description: Parse JSON into an internal JSONValue enum (object/array/string/number/bool/null). Use Foundation JSONSerialization for the parse, but wrap it so we can attach line/col positions on parse failure (NSError userInfo from JSONSerialization carries this in JSONSerialization domain). Performance budget: 50MB JSON parses in <1s on M-series. Holds the parsed tree for tree-render to walk.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #83: custom recursive-descent JSON parser. Ordered keys, int/double preserved, line/col errors. 50MB parses in 0.29s release. 26 tests.

title: data-viewer.format-detect
description: Route .json and .jsonl files to a new DataViewer path instead of MarkdownDocument. Hook into the same open-file pipeline (AppDelegate.handleOpenFile → MainWindow.handleLaunchURL / .peekOpenFile notification). Decide format from extension; trust the extension (no content sniffing in v1). Plain text fallback for malformed JSON: render parse error inline with line/col indicator, don't crash.

title: search.content-search
description: Cmd+Shift+F: synchronous scan with cancellation, line + context preview, 10k-file cap for v1.
close_reason: ContentSearcher: detached-Task scan with 150ms debounce + cancel-on-restart, 10k file cap, 500KB per-file cap, 200 result cap. Cmd+Shift+F opens palette in .content mode; results render as 'matched line' / 'path:lineNumber'. Activation opens the file (line-jump deferred — needs source→DOM mapping). 10 new tests.

title: search.fuzzy-files
description: File-name match across the open folder. Subsequence scoring with CamelCase boost. Rebuild index on folder open — no background indexer per CLAUDE.md.
close_reason: FuzzyMatch (subsequence + word-start + contiguous + substring bonuses) and FileIndex (async generation-guarded walk, ignored dir set, md-only / show-all modes) wired into MainWindow. Cmd+P now ranks files by score against the relative path; top 50 results. Index rebuilds on folder open + showAllFiles toggle. 18 new tests (10 FuzzyMatch, 8 FileIndex).

title: search.palette-shell
description: Cmd+P overlay, keyboard-only, visually consistent with FindBar. Foundation for fuzzy + content search.
close_reason: CommandPalette overlay (Cmd+P → 'Go to File…'). Centered modal, query field + results list, kbd nav (Up/Down, Ctrl+N/P, Esc, Return), click-outside dismiss. PaletteItem data model with id/title/subtitle/icon. Empty-state messages route by folder-open + query state. Print moved to ⇧⌘P to free ⌘P. Wired into MainWindow + NotificationBridge via .peekPaletteOpen.

title: tree-perf.subtree-invalidation
description: FSEvents callback gives us changed paths; only rebuild the affected subtree, not the whole tree. Stretch goal — needs lazy children + a node cache.

title: roadmap.fix-architecture-md
description: ARCHITECTURE.md:65 still says 'Not an editor-first tool. Editing is a later sprint and will remain secondary to viewing.' Reframe — editing is not a sprint at all anymore.
close_reason: Roadmap reorganized in CLAUDE.md (already on main via bd init commit), README.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md: editor sprint dropped, data viewers moved to v0.5, themes to v0.6.

title: roadmap.drop-editor
description: peek is a viewer, not an editor. Remove the editor sprint from CLAUDE.md and README.md roadmaps; add explicit 'no editor sprint' line.
close_reason: Roadmap reorganized in CLAUDE.md (already on main via bd init commit), README.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md: editor sprint dropped, data viewers moved to v0.5, themes to v0.6.

title: roadmap.move-data-viewers-to-v0.5
description: Data viewers (JSON/JSONL/YAML/TOML) ship after Search, before Config. Reorder roadmap in CLAUDE.md and README.md.
close_reason: Roadmap reorganized in CLAUDE.md (already on main via bd init commit), README.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md: editor sprint dropped, data viewers moved to v0.5, themes to v0.6.

title: fs-watch.manual-refresh
description: Add File → Refresh Folder (Cmd+Shift+R) menu item + peekRefreshFolder notification + bridge wiring as a manual fallback for when FSEvents misses (network mounts, etc.).
close_reason: Implemented FSEvents-based FolderWatcher; FolderBrowser now auto-refreshes on disk changes. Cmd+Shift+R wired as manual fallback.

title: search.perf-budget
description: Results visible within one frame on a 10k-file folder. Record a benchmark.
close_reason: SearchPerfTests pins the v0.4 perf claims: walk (5k files / 0.25s), fuzzy scoring per keystroke (5k candidates / 30ms), content scan (5k files / 1s). All 3 pass locally with significant headroom; CI failures here are a regression signal.

title: search.unified-palette
description: Single palette for files + content; mode toggle by prefix or hotkey.
close_reason: Tab toggles between Files/Content modes inside the palette; visible mode badge with ⇥ hint, also clickable. Switching mode wipes query (since the query semantics change between modes — fuzzy file match vs content substring scan).

title: roadmap.relabel-config-issues
description: GH issues #24–#28 carry sprint:v0.5 but are config/themes — relabel to sprint:v0.6 to match new roadmap.
close_reason: Closed GH #20–23 (editor sprint), relabeled GH #24–28 from sprint:v0.5 to sprint:v0.6 (config/themes); created sprint:v0.6 label.

title: roadmap.close-editor-issues
description: Close GH issues #20–#23 (split-view editor, NSTextView wrapper, autosave, sync scroll) — editor sprint is dropped.
close_reason: Closed GH #20–23 (editor sprint), relabeled GH #24–28 from sprint:v0.5 to sprint:v0.6 (config/themes); created sprint:v0.6 label.
